title: Bidens mannii
intro: Bidens mannii, also known as Mann's beggar lice, is a plant species belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). Traditionally, Bidens mannii is used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antimalarial properties, providing potential health benefits such as wound healing, fever reduction, and parasite control. The bioactive constituents of Bidens mannii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, including bidensmin, which has been shown to exhibit antimalarial activity. Bidens mannii is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are traditionally used to treat various ailments, particularly in tropical regions.
taxonomy: Bidens mannii is classified as a species within the genus Bidens, which is a group of plants in the family Asteraceae. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics and shared traits with other plants in the same genus. Bidens mannii is further classified as a member of the order Asterales, a group of flowering plants that includes daisies and sunflowers. At the kingdom level, it falls under Plantae, which includes all plant species. The classification of Bidens mannii provides important information about its evolutionary history and relationships with other plants.
uses: Bidens mannii is used in traditional medicine, particularly in Ethiopia, to treat various ailments. The plant is valued for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are believed to help soothe wounds and reduce pain. It is also used to treat fever and respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds. In addition, Bidens mannii is thought to have antibacterial and antifungal properties, making it useful for treating infections. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses and potential benefits of Bidens mannii.
